Talks and conference sessions

Making complex technology more explicit, practical, and human.

I speak about agentic software engineering, multi-agent systems, and attention under pressure. The technical and reflective topics share one aim: helping people create clarity, retain judgment, and work together with more confidence.

View my Sessionize profile Connect on LinkedIn
Featured talk

A practical model for agentic engineering.

Presented Advanced · AI-assisted development

Engineering Triad

The Agentic Developer

Stop with Vibe Coding and Master the Engineering Triad

Also presented as The Agentic Engineer: Making Implicit Engineering Explicit. These are two titles for the same session.

Generated code is not delivered work. This talk introduces a practical model for making agent contributions reliable, observable, and safe by making three parts of engineering explicit: intent, context, and execution.

Using a password-reset flow as a running example, the session moves from vague prompting to durable specifications, BDD scenarios, repository rules, focused agent roles, validation gates, and human acceptance.

Audience takeaway: when execution becomes cheap, clarity becomes the engineering bottleneck. Do not only fix an agent's output; improve the environment that produced it.

Conference talks

Developed, presented, and ready for a new audience.

These sessions have already been given at conferences and communities. They can be adapted to the room while keeping their practical core.

01
Presented Advanced · Multi-agent systems

Orchestrating a Game Dev Team Using Microsoft Agent Framework

What happens when I want to build a game but do not have a studio? I build the studio from AI agents. This practical case follows specialised Game Designer, Developer, QA, Producer, and Reviewer agents working on one product.

Takeaway: design clear roles, shared artifacts, review gates, and stop criteria so multi-agent speed does not become multi-agent chaos, and understand where the human engineer remains indispensable.

02
Presented Beginner · Mindfulness for developers

Working Like a Buddha: Less Stress, More Joy

Software developers move constantly between code, chat, meetings, reviews, incidents, and production. This down-to-earth session translates Buddhist principles such as attention, simplicity, and presence into ordinary engineering work, without incense or vague promises.

Takeaway: recognise the cost of context switching and use small, practical habits to bring more focus, calm, and enjoyment to coding, reviews, meetings, and incidents.

Invite me to speak

Conference, meetup, or an engineering team that wants to go deeper.

The sessions can be adapted to the audience, time slot, and technical depth. Each one connects concrete engineering practice with the human conditions needed to make it work. My Sessionize profile contains the current talk information; LinkedIn is the easiest place to start a conversation.
